frameworks.name: intuitive physics
frameworks.one_line: everyday physical prediction grounded in mental approximations of object dynamics

frameworks.name: approximate object representation
frameworks.one_line: dual body-vs-shape representations dissociated for action vs recognition

frameworks.name: physical reasoning
frameworks.one_line: fast everyday inference about how objects will behave

core_question: Do people rely on simplified body approximations rather than fine-grained shapes when reasoning about physical behavior of objects?
key_assumptions: physical prediction must run fast enough for real-time action
tracking/action and recognition can use different object representations
engineers' simplified-collider approximations are a useful model class for the mind
manipulations: task domain across three classic psychophysics paradigms (causality, collision detection, change detection)
stimulus geometry dissociating body (collider) from fine-grained shape
comparison range spanning convex hulls through detailed shapes
key_findings: behavior across all three tasks reflects approximate body representations rather than fine-grained shapes
the operative approximation lies between convex hulls and full fine-grained shapes
body-based predictions hold across causality, collision, and change-detection settings
interpretations: physical reasoning uses a principled simplified body representation distinct from the shape used for recognition
the mind employs engineering-style approximations for real-time physical simulation
